Error Codes

Every Kraken API endpoint returns standard HTTP status codes. Authentication failures surface as 401; missing permissions as 403; missing resources as 404. Rate-limit exhaustion surfaces as 429 with the X-Rate-Limit-Retry-After header described in the rate-limit documentation.

HTTP status codes

  • 200 — request succeeded
  • 400 — malformed request body or query parameters
  • 401 — missing or invalid API key
  • 403 — key valid but lacks the requested permission
  • 404 — resource does not exist
  • 429 — rate-limit exceeded; check Retry-After header
  • 500 — internal server error; retry with backoff

Kraken error codes

On any 4xx response, the JSON body includes a Kraken-specific error_code field. Codes are documented below.

  • KRK-001 — invalid API key format
  • KRK-002 — API key expired (rotate via dashboard)
  • KRK-003 — OAuth token expired (refresh with the refresh token)
  • KRK-101 — request body missing required field
  • KRK-201 — rate limit exceeded for this account tier
